title: Markowitz Mean-Variance Portfolio Optimization
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Markowitz Mean-Variance Portfolio Optimization: Markowitz portfolio: find minimum variance portfolio for target return using covariance matrix from historical data. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.covariance.sample_shrinkage…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~50; estimation_error_expected_returns, covariance_matrix_instability set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: min_w w ** T Sigma w s.t. mu ** T w = mu_target, 1 ** T w = 1, w >= 0
E.world_state_x: optimal portfolio weights w in R ** N satisfying constraints
E.observation_y: historical return matrix R in R^{T x N} for N assets over T periods

W.regime: Existence of the recovered portfolio_weight_vector is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 50); estimation_error_expected_returns dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Estimation gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: out-of-sample Sharpe > 0.5 for 1-year lookback. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~50. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |estimation_error_expected_returns|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N ** 2 * T) for covariance estimation + O(N ** 3) for QP per iteration
